questions.question:

If the cost of computing g(x) is min{no. of leaf-nodes in left-subtree of x, no. of leaf-nodes in right-subtree of x} then the worst-case time complexity of the program is

(GATE CSE 2004)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following C program segment

struct CellNode
{
  struct CelINode *leftchild;
  int element;
  struct CelINode *rightChild;
}
 
int Dosomething(struct CelINode *ptr)
{
    int value = 0;
    if (ptr != NULL)
    {
      if (ptr->leftChild != NULL)
        value = 1 + DoSomething(ptr->leftChild);
      if (ptr->rightChild != NULL)
        value = max(value, 1 + DoSomething(ptr->rightChild));
    }
    return (value);
}
The value returned by the function DoSomething when a pointer to the root of a non-empty tree is passed as argument is

(GATE CSE 2004)
options:
A.

The number of leaf nodes in the tree

B.

The number of nodes in the tree

C.

The number of internal nodes in the tree
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the function f defined below.


struct item
{
    int data;
    struct item * next;
};
int f(struct item *p)
{
    return ((p == NULL) || (p->next == NULL) ||
            ((P->data <= p->next->data) &&
            f(p->next)));
}
For a given linked list p, the function f returns 1 if and only if

(GATE CSE 2003)
options:
A.

the list is empty or has exactly one element

B.

the elements in the list are sorted in non-decreasing order of data value

C.

the elements in the list are sorted in non-increasing order of data value

D.

not all elements in the list have the same data value
questions.correct_answer:
B.

the elements in the list are sorted in non-decreasing order of data value
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
